image:image.image:title: Why teams reject AI that feels like replacement
image:image.image:caption: AI adoption usually fails for a human reason. When the system feels like control or replacement, teams resist it. When it supports autonomy, competence, and visible wins, usage rises.

image:image.image:title: Adoption fails when it feels like replacement
image:image.image:caption: AI adoption usually fails for a human reason. When the system feels like control or replacement, teams resist it. When it supports autonomy, competence, and visible wins, usage rises.

image:image.image:title: When a leaderboard becomes surveillance
image:image.image:caption: Leaderboards can create short-term movement, but in workplace learning they often drift into surveillance. The better design question is whether the system strengthens autonomy, competence, and useful feedback.
